source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
During a cycle race starting at $D$ and finishing at $B$ every connecting road (between the towns $A, B, C$ and $D$ ) that is shown in the diagram will be ridden along exactly once. How many possible routes are there for the race?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says roads are 'shown in the diagram'
the needed road connectivity among A, B, C, D is not specified in text alone
multi_step: solver must first recover the graph/road connections from the image
solver must then count valid routes from D to B using each shown road exactly once
this is a constrained graph-counting task rather than direct lookup
verifiability: question asks for a finite count of possible routes, which is objectively checkable once the graph is identified
image exists and has acceptable readability_score=0.8073, supporting downstream verification

risk_flags: graph structure is only available via the image
route count may depend on exact interpretation of the diagram if any road intersections or parallel edges are visually ambiguous

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are complete
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
image-dependency, multi-step, and verifiability scores are all high
although the graph structure is image-dependent and may have some interpretation risk, the image exists and appears sufficiently readable, so this is suitable for the candidate pool
